req.title: Bill pay (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall pay a registered biller between $1 and $50,000 with deterministic settlement date calculation based on biller cut-off. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-032 defines a bill pay endpoint with an amount range [$1, $50000] and deterministic settlement date logic. Test design covers: (1) EP positive — mid-range valid amount to confirm happy path scheduling and settlement date presence; (2) NEG — amount below minimum ($0.99) triggers 422; (3) BVA lower bound — exact $1.00 accepted; (4) BVA upper bound — exact $50000.00 accepted; (5) NEG — amount above maximum ($50000.01) triggers 422. This covers EP, BVA, and NEG heuristics across the stated error pathways and expected outcomes.

tests.name: test_billpay_valid_mid_range_amount
preconditions: Biller is registered in the system
actions: POST /billpay with amount=500.00 and a registered biller_id
expected: Response body contains status indicating payment scheduled
trace: R-FIN-032: payment scheduled; settlement date returned
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import date

def test_billpay_valid_mid_range_amount(base_url, auth_headers, registered_biller_id):
    payload = {
        "biller_id": registered_biller_id,
        "amount": "500.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/billpay",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201)
    body = resp.json()
    assert "payment_id" in body, "Missing payment_id"
    assert body.get("settlement_date") is not None, "Missing settlement_date"
    settlement = body["settlement_date"]
    parsed = date.fromisoformat(settlement)
    assert parsed >= date.today(), "Settlement date must not be in the past"
    assert body.get("status") in ("SCHEDULED", "PENDING", "ACCEPTED")

tests.name: test_billpay_amount_below_minimum_returns_422
preconditions: Biller is registered in the system
expected: Response body contains error detail referencing amount constraint
trace: R-FIN-032: 422 when amount out of range
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_billpay_amount_below_minimum_returns_422(base_url, auth_headers, registered_biller_id):
    payload = {
        "biller_id": registered_biller_id,
        "amount": "0.99",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/billpay",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, (
        f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    error_text = str(body).lower()
    assert "amount" in error_text, "Error detail must reference 'amount'"

trace: R-FIN-032: boundary behaviour at exact min threshold must be tested
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import date

def test_billpay_exact_minimum_boundary_accepted(base_url, auth_headers, registered_biller_id):
    payload = {
        "biller_id": registered_biller_id,
        "amount": "1.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/billpay",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), (
        f"Expected 2xx at lower bound, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert "payment_id" in body
    assert body.get("settlement_date") is not None
    date.fromisoformat(body["settlement_date"])

tests.name: test_billpay_amount_above_maximum_returns_422
preconditions: Biller is registered in the system
actions: POST /billpay with amount=50000.01 (one cent above $50,000 maximum)
expected: Response body contains error detail referencing amount constraint
trace: R-FIN-032: 422 when amount out of range
BVA: one unit above upper boundary

tests.name: test_billpay_settlement_date_respects_biller_cutoff
preconditions: Biller is registered with a known cut-off time (e.g. 17:00 EST)
System clock is mocked or request timestamp is controlled via <mock_clock_fixture>
actions: POST /billpay with amount=250.00 submitted BEFORE biller cut-off
POST /billpay with amount=250.00 submitted AFTER biller cut-off (via <mock_clock_fixture>)
expected: T1 settlement_date equals next business day N
T2 settlement_date equals business day N+1 (cut-off crossed adds one day)
trace: R-FIN-032: deterministic settlement date calculation based on biller cut-off
DT: decision table — rows: before cut-off / after cut-off; outcome: settlement day shifts
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import date, timedelta

def test_billpay_settlement_date_respects_biller_cutoff(
    base_url, auth_headers, registered_biller_id,
    mock_clock_before_cutoff, mock_clock_after_cutoff,
    next_business_day
):
    payload = {"biller_id": registered_biller_id, "amount": "250.00", "currency": "USD"}

    with mock_clock_before_cutoff():
        r1 = requests.post(f"{base_url}/billpay", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert r1.status_code in (200, 201)
    t1 = date.fromisoformat(r1.json()["settlement_date"])

    with mock_clock_after_cutoff():
        r2 = requests.post(f"{base_url}/billpay", json=payload, headers=auth_headers)
    assert r2.status_code in (200, 201)
    t2 = date.fromisoformat(r2.json()["settlement_date"])

    nbd = next_business_day(date.today())
    assert t1 == nbd, f"Before cut-off: expected {nbd}, got {t1}"
    assert t2 > t1, "After cut-off settlement must be later than before cut-off"
